name: Jagannath Temple
description: One of the Char Dhams and home to Lord Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra. Famous for the annual Rath Yatra chariot festival.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: 13th-century UNESCO World Heritage chariot-shaped temple dedicated to the Sun God. Masterpiece of Kalinga architecture with intricate carvings.

name: Puri Beach
description: One of India's finest beaches along the Bay of Bengal. Golden sands stretch for miles, popular for sunrise and sand art festivals.

name: Chilika Lake
description: Asia's largest brackish water lagoon and a Ramsar wetland. Home to Irrawaddy dolphins and over a million migratory birds in winter.

name: Gundicha Temple
description: Destination temple of the annual Rath Yatra. Lord Jagannath visits his aunt Gundicha for 9 days. Known as the Garden House of Lord.

name: Lingaraja Temple
description: Largest and most important temple of Bhubaneswar, dating to 11th century. 180-feet deul tower is the finest example of Odisha architecture.

name: Chandrabhaga Beach
description: Pristine beach 3 km from Konark temple. Named after a river, famous for the Chandrabhaga Mela and Asia's first Blue Flag beach.

name: Raghurajpur Heritage Village
description: India's heritage crafts village where every household practices Pattachitra painting, palm leaf art, and stone carving.

name: Markandeshwar Temple
description: Ancient Shiva temple near Jagannath Temple. One of the oldest temples in Puri with beautiful carvings and peaceful ambiance.

name: Sakshi Gopal Temple
description: Temple housing the witness (Sakshi) form of Lord Gopal (Krishna) who walked here to testify for a poor Brahmin. Unique folklore.

name: Pipili Applique Village
description: Famous applique work village on the Puri highway. Colorful fabric crafts, umbrellas, and canopies made for Rath Yatra.

name: Swargadwar
description: The sacred cremation ground of Puri meaning 'Gateway to Heaven'. Hindus believe cremation here ensures liberation.

name: Narendra Pokhari
description: Large sacred tank where Chandan Yatra festival is celebrated. The deities of Jagannath Temple are taken for a boat ride here.

name: Lokanath Temple
description: Ancient Shiva temple where the lingam is submerged in water and only visible during Shivaratri. One of Puri's most revered sites.

name: Atharnala Bridge
description: 13th-century stone bridge with 18 arches. Believed to be the point where Rath Yatra chariots were historically constructed.

name: Dhauli Peace Pagoda
description: White peace pagoda at the site of Kalinga War (261 BC) where Emperor Ashoka embraced Buddhism. Rock edicts still visible.

name: Mukteshwar Temple
description: 10th-century gem of Odishan architecture. The ornate gateway torana arch is one of the finest carved arches in India.

name: Sudarshan Crafts Museum
description: Museum showcasing Odisha's traditional stone and wood carving heritage. Live demonstrations by master artisans.

name: Beleswar Beach
description: Serene black sand beach near Konark. Less crowded with a small Shiva temple on the shore. Beautiful sunset views.

name: Alarnath Temple
description: Temple where devotees worship during Anasar period when Jagannath Temple is closed for 15 days. Rare 4-armed Vishnu deity.
